title: CACOON - 211info
text: LINN COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH ALBANY CACOON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Public health nurse home visiting program that provides support to families with children and youth with special health care needs. Home visitors help families coordinate care for their children, connect with healthcare, community support, and make sure their child’s health team works together. Helps prepare older youth with special health needs for the transition to adulthood. 1050 SW 7th Avenue Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Families with children and youth ages 0-21 with chronic health or developmental conditions Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-noon and 1pm-5pm Email (541) 967-3888 2489 . Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers (541) 967-3888 Main phone (541) 926-2102 FAX (800) 735-1232 TTY/TTD (800) 304-7468 Toll-free Main phone (541) 967-3888 ext 2489 Laura Jimenez [email protected] Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake LANGUAGES: English, Spanish AREA SERVED: Linn County TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Services: Case/Care Management * Children and Youth With Disabilities/Health Conditions Home Based Parenting Education * Children and Youth With Disabilities/Health Conditions Home/Community Based Developmental Disabilities Programs Specialized Information and Referral * Children and Youth With Disabilities/Health Conditions Other Services Offered At This Location: HEALTH SUPPORTIVE SERVICES - HIV/AIDS RAPID TESTING AND PREVENTION HEALTH SUPPORTIVE SERVICES - OREGON MOTHERSCARE INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - BABIES FIRST! INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - FAMILY CONNECTS INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - NURSE FAMILY PARTNERSHIP PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - WOMEN, INFANTS, AND CHILDREN SUPPLEMENTAL NUTRITION PROGRAM SPECIALIZED TREATMENT AND PREVENTION - TUBERCULOSIS PROGRAM SPECIALIZED TREATMENT AND PREVENTION - VACCINES AND IMMUNIZATION If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
